page: Vultr vc2-2c-4gb is a Cloud Compute server configured with 2 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs operating at 2.0 GHz, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 80 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The x86_64 architecture features 2 physical cores with 1 thread per core, 16 MB of L3 cache, and supports instruction sets including AVX, AVX512F, and AES. It includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks position this instance in the lower tiers. The stress-ng single-core score is 1226.09 and the multi-core score is 2501.8, both falling in the bottom 25%. Memory performance is constrained, with a large-block read bandwidth of 19.26 GB/sec and write bandwidth of 16.87 GB/sec. Database and web serving capabilities are also limited, as indicated by a Passmark database score of 374.96 and static web serving throughput of 1.8 GB/sec.

Given its shared CPU allocation and modest performance metrics, this server offers a highly cost-efficient entry point for resource-light workloads. It is best suited for basic web serving, development environments, lightweight testing, and small-scale background tasks that do not require sustained high-performance compute or memory resources.
description: Vultr vc2-2c-4gb is a budget-oriented Cloud Compute server featuring 2 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs at 2.0 GHz, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 80 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance is designed for entry-level workloads. Benchmark results place its compute and memory capabilities in the lower tiers, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1226.09 and a Passmark CPU score of 2470.92. The server lacks dedicated GPU acceleration. Given its shared CPU allocation and modest resource density, it represents a highly cost-efficient option for non-critical applications. It is best suited for basic web serving, lightweight testing, and development environments.
